title: Falls review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports asking about the details of a fall when a covered person presents after one and explicitly rejects automatic prediction-tool use.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact section supports interval fall details, onset, change, functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and concerns without inferring cause.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports asking about the details of a fall when a covered person presents after one and explicitly rejects automatic prediction-tool use.

source.title: Falls: assessment and prevention in older people and in people 50 and over at higher risk

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.5 — identifying people for falls assessment

examination.text: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.
examination.rationale: The exact comprehensive assessment section supports clinician-assessed cardiovascular, cognitive, dizziness, footwear, functional, gait, hearing, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence, vision and nutrition domains.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

source.title: Falls: assessment and prevention in older people and in people 50 and over at higher risk

sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE pathway adaptation

source_limitations: Injury, acute illness, syncope, environment, actual examination, diagnosis, investigation, treatment, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
